rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JANE DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Site Inspection, Camera Inspection
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 16, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JANE DOE'S MOTION To Compel Site Inspection, Camera Inspection Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to appear to discuss whether there is good cause for inspection of office that has been changed in light to allegations in complaint and to address issues associated with examination of the electronic storage device, why, if such discovery is ordered, plaintiff should not be required to pay for this discovery and how to address the privacy rights of other patients. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MARIA GARCIA CASTILLO VS. LUIS HUMBERTO ROMERO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 16, 2013, Line 5: DEFENDANT LUIS ROMERO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether Defendants Romero and Rivas were driving the vehicle that struck Plaintiff at the corner of 24th Street and Alabama Street on January 14, 2012. Mr. Medina's affidavit in support of Defendant's reply is inadmissible as it does not comply with CCP § 2015.5. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/AJR

rulings.Case Title: JOSE CARPIO et al VS. MARITA TAYLAN ALBANO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 16, 2013, Line 7: DEFENDANT AVIS BUDGET CAR RENTAL, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant acted reasonably by relying on Ms. Albano's driver's license and plaintiff fails to demonstrate that defendant acted negligently or engaged in criminal wrongdoing within the meaning of the Graves Amendment (49 USC 30106). Defendant correctly notes that this action involves liability, not insurance coverage or financial responsibility laws. rulings.Case Title: NETSANET HAILEMICHAEL VS. BANK OF THE WEST, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition Of Michael Bracco, Senior Executive Vice President And Chief Administrative Officer For Bank Of The West, For Protective Order Re Same, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,500
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 16, 2013, Line 10: DEFENDANT BANK OF THE WEST'S Motion To Quash Notice Of Deposition Of Michael Bracco, Senior Executive Vice President And Chief Administrative Officer For Bank Of The West, For Protective Order Re Same, And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $2,500 REVISED [DEC-13-2013, 4:08PM] Tentative Ruling: Denied. A plaintiff need not exhaust other methods of discovery when the apex individual has unique or superior personal knowledge of discoverable information. (Liberty Mutual Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal. App. 4th 1282, 1289.) , The undisputed evidence is that Plaintiff sent an email to Bank of the West's CEO, Michael Shepherd (who she had previously met) complaining that she was resigning from the bank because "[f]or several months I had to endure a barrage of insults and sexual harassment from Nolen Boyer . . . ." Mr. Shepherd forwarded her email to Michael Bracco with instructions to "Please look into these comments . . . . If you find they are substantiated, please brief me and make any recommendations you believe appropriate." (Stephenson Decl. in Opposition to Motion to Quash, Exhibit K). Mr. Bracco has unique or superior personal knowledge of discoverable information, and Plaintiff is entitled to take Mr. Bracco's deposition. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/AJR

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 16, 2013, Line 16: DEFENDANT PROPANE STUDIO LLC'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint Granted. Cross-complainants shall file the proposed cross-complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. The Court finds the proposed cross-complaint is compulsory and therefore governed by CCP § 426.50. CCP § 426.50 has been interpreted as requiring the Court to grant leave to file a compulsory cross-complaint absent bad faith. (Silver Organizations Ltd. v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 98-99.) The law strongly favors granting leave, as compulsory claims are barred if not pled in the underlying case. (Sidney v. Sup. Ct. (1988) 198 Cal.App.3d 710, 718.) The principle of liberality in CCP § 426.50 "requires that a strong showing of bad faith be made in order to support a denial of the right to file a cross-complaint¿[i]t is preferable that the parties have their day in court." (Id.) Plaintiffs have not met the very high burden of establishing bad faith here. Delay in filing a cross-complaint is not sufficient to establish bad faith, unless the filing of the cross-complaint would "work a substantial injustice to the opposing party and would prejudice the opposing party's position in some way." (Foot's Transfer & Storage Co. v. Superior Court. (1980) 114 Cal.App.3d 897, 903.) The evidence does not establish substantial injustice or prejudice here. (See e.g., Gherman v. Colburn (1977) 72 Cal.App.3d 544, where the court found bad faith because the evidence suggesting that the cross-complainant's purpose for filing the cross-complaint was to prevent a jury trial amounted to substantial injustice.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/AJR

rulings.Case Title: ONE BLOCK OFF THE GRID INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. JEFF RICHARDS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And To Compel Defts To Produce Records And To Provide Substative Written Disocveyr Repsonses To Requests For Production, Specal And Form Interrogatories And Request For Admission & Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 16, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF ONE BLOCK OFF THE GRID INC, A DELAWARE CORPORATION (Amended) Notice Of Motion And To Compel Defts To Produce Records And To Provide Substative Written Disocveyr Repsonses To Requests For Production, Specal And Form Interrogatories And Request For Admission & Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Robert Kane, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted no oppositions. Sanctions denied as CCP §§2030.300(d) and 2033.3290(d) that were relied upon by moving party requires opposition to be filed and none was filed but granted per CCP §2023.030(a) in amount of $1060 for reasonable costs of preparation of motion to compel where no responses were made. (See also CRC 1.1030.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rkane1089@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: LINDA A HESS VS. EXPERIAN CREDIT REPORTING INFO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 16, 2013, Line 21: DEFENDANTS GE CAPITAL MONEY BANK'S (FKA GE MONEY BANK) and GECRB'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer to the 1st cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead the cause of action against defendant defendants and highlight what section of the Consumer Reporting Agencies Act she seeks to recover under. In addition, if plaintiff seeks relief under the Investigative Consumer Reporting Agencies Act-a separate statutory scheme-plaintiff should allege that claim as a distinct cause of action. The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege an actual controversy. The Court sustains with 20 days leave to amend the demurrer to the accounting cause of action. To state a cause of action, only the simplest pleading is required: (1) The fiduciary relationship or other circumstances appropriate to the remedy [citation], [and] (2) A balance due from the defendant to the plaintiff that can only be ascertained by an accounting." (5 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Pleading, § 820, p. 236.) Plaintiff does not allege that a balance is due from Defendant to Plaintiff. (Complaint, 17.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
